title: --mount=type=cache permissions issue *in case of nested folders* with non-root users
body:
### Issue Description

The `--mount=type=cache,uid=1000,gid=1000` apply very restrictive permissions to all folders created except the last one.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Build the following containerfile:
```dockerfile
FROM debian:latest

# Create user
RUN useradd -m --uid 1000 build
USER 1000

RUN --mount=type=cache,uid=1000,gid=1000,target=/foo/bar ls -ald /foo /foo/bar
```

Notice the different behaviour in podman and docker (/foo having different permissions)

### Describe the results you received

podman:
```
ls: cannot access '/foo/bar': Permission denied
drwx------ 1 root root 6 Jan  1  1970 /foo
```
docker:
```
#7 0.147 drwxr-xr-x 3 root  root  4096 Sep 20 18:54 /foo
#7 0.147 drwxr-xr-x 2 build build 4096 Sep 20 18:46 /foo/bar
```

### Describe the results you expected

See above

### podman info output

comments.body:
Possible duplicate of: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27044

---

To workaround `Permission denied` issue with nested `--volume` in `podman build`, I've downgraded to `5.5.2` since current git doesn't appear to ship the updated Buildah [submodule](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/167102951749ebe140de76bd049a4df53f437a7c/go.mod#L14) yet (https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6389). Although the relevant [change](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6381) is already merged to buildah's [`release-1.41`](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/ee5b5742b0c5f8c879b140146b93971bb6a0d385) branch, it haven't been released either (I believe it will land in the upcoming Buildah update: `1.41.5`)

Example for Arch Linux if you don't have the binary:
```bash
su build
git clone https://gitlab.archlinux.org/archlinux/packaging/packages/podman.git
git checkout da6b34117e29b302ca02627ecbb2c7983736dbcd
cd podman/
gpg --keyserver hkps://keys.openpgp.org --recv-keys 5A0F5FA1CEF5C874
PACMAN=paru makepkg --syncdeps --needed --install
```

Later on you should be able to confirm that you've got the update with:
```bash
podman info | grep buildah
```
Looks like a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27044, should be fixed in v5.6.2
I can confirm the fix, thx
